summary: override: accept codex [P2] as an upstream verbatim-tool property; no fix-N (converge, don't fork)
body: The P2 is a pre-existing characteristic of the verbatim-copied memex ledgerwatch tool (107 tests green incl. test_jsonl_inplace_rewrite_same_size_detected). Patching here would fork the tool from its memex source of truth, violating the converge-don't-fork directive. Recorded for the tool's upstream owner (memex) instead. Same posture for the illustrative dev/steward/DESIGN.md reference in the verbatim ledgerwrite README. Neither is a BLOCK.

HITL STANDING MANDATE (2026-07-05): the repo MUST use the 3090 GPUs for eval/embed/rerank internal activities (eu7 harness, re-embeds, sweeps) when there's compute+memory room; CPU fallback only. Triggered by finding the fresh eu7 run on the CPU embedder (--features default-embedder, ~0.43 docs/sec, seed-timeout) with BOTH 3090s at 0% idle. GPU=15-93x faster (0.8.7), CPU<->CUDA 1-bit identical => fidelity-safe for the eu7 0.90 gate. ACTIONS: (1) directed the live orchestrator to abort the CPU eu7 run + re-run on GPU (embed-cuda + FATHOMDB_EMBED_DEVICE=cuda:0, exclude K620, verify engaged); (2) durable capture -- repo policy doc dev/design/gpu-eval-activities-policy.md (scoped: internal eval/dev/CI, NOT the shipped CPU/opt-in default), memory repo-must-use-gpu-for-eval-when-3090-room, MEMORY.md index; (3) TC-4 = the tooling-enforcement work item (harness/eval-runner DEFAULT to GPU-when-room, room-check + CPU fallback), p1, placement TBD (0.8.14 Slice40 or fast-follow). Decider: HITL (mandate), Steward (apply + capture + track).

GPU-for-eval mandate REFINED (HITL 2026-07-05): CPU is NOT a general fallback for GPU-able work. Full eval/embed/rerank suites ALWAYS run on GPU; CPU permitted ONLY for two narrow probes -- (1) simple CPU-compatibility testing, (2) CPU-library repeatability/compatibility checks. 'Feature compatibility' = check compat with CPU libraries, NOT run full suites on BOTH CPU and GPU; never mirror a full suite onto CPU. When 3090s busy: wait/queue or use the other 3090, not a full CPU run. Updated policy doc (Rule + new 'CPU only for compatibility probes' section + How + Enforcement), memory, TC-4 (refinement entry). HITL also CONFIRMED the scope-lock: mandate governs internal eval/dev/CI only; shipped-library default (CPU/opt-in, footprint invariant) UNCHANGED. Decider: HITL (refine), Steward (capture).
